domains.name: Qualisign
hints: A pure quality — the feeling of red before red is named
First contact with raw phenomenal texture
The immediate apprehension of tone without content
What anesthesia or deep presence work touches
The qualisign is a possibility, not an actual thing
Resists parsing; it must be felt to be known
In healing, the threshold between sensation and perception
The unit-ground from which all semiosis emerges

domains.name: Sinsign
hints: An actually existing thing or event — this mark, that sound
Singular, here-now, unrepeatable in identical form
The brute facticity of a particular instance
What scaffolding or prototyping produces: one real thing
Indexes by virtue of being a physical trace
Every construction begins as a sinsign before it becomes a type
The unit of evidence in a singular observation
Grounds the walk through a specific site of material resistance

domains.name: Legisign
hints: A sign that is a law, convention, or type — the word 'dog' understood
Not the utterance but the rule that governs all utterances
The binding structure that makes communication predictable
What makes a social bond durable across instances
Legisigns are general; they require replicas to be actualized
The domain of grammar, code, and contract
Creation through the application of a generative rule
Without legisigns, no shared meaning can be built

domains.name: Icon
hints: A sign that denotes by virtue of resemblance — a portrait, diagram, metaphor
Shares qualities with its object; imitates what it represents
The basis of all visual representation and analogical thinking
What fabrication draws on when shaping form to match idea
Icons are indifferent to whether their object actually exists
Every model or blueprint is iconic before it becomes indexical
The mode of diagrams, maps, and prototypes
Creation through likeness — making the invisible visible by resemblance

domains.name: Index
hints: A sign that denotes by factual connection — smoke for fire, footprint for foot
Points to its object by being really affected by it
The evidential backbone of empirical inquiry
What research walks toward: causes, correlates, traces
Indices are existentially connected; destroy the object and the sign dies
A weathervane indexes wind direction without describing it
Every measurement instrument produces indexical signs
The study mode of clues, symptoms, and physical evidence

domains.name: Symbol
hints: A sign that denotes by habit, law, or convention — most words are symbols
Arbitrary and learned, not naturally connected to its object
The substrate of all cultural and institutional meaning
What binds a community through shared interpretive habits
Symbols grow: they acquire new meanings through use over time
A flag is a symbol; its power comes from collective agreement
Learning a language is acquiring a symbol system
Without symbols, there is no abstract thought or legal bond

domains.name: Rheme
hints: A sign of qualitative possibility — a term like '___ is red'
The interpretant is an open potential, not a judgment
What fabrication lifts from possibility into provisional form
The rheme is the least determined interpretant; a mere predicate
In design, the first sketch that could become anything
The semiotic unit of brainstorming and divergent thinking
A rheme asserts nothing; it merely presents a quality for consideration
The mode of material possibility — what this substance could signify

domains.name: Dicisign
hints: A sign of actual existence — a proposition like 'This stone is heavy'
Makes a claim about the world that can be true or false
The interpretant asserts something of an object
What construction work verifies: does this joint hold?
Dicisigns are the atoms of factual discourse
A photograph is dicisign: it asserts that its subject existed
The mode of specification, dimensioning, and engineering judgment
Work that decides between true and false, fit and unfit

domains.name: Argument
hints: A sign that represents its object through reasoned inference
Not a single proposition but a structure of reasoning from premises to conclusion
The highest, most mediated form of semeiotic interpretant
What scholarly study produces: logical arguments from evidence
Arguments are the only signs that deliberately address their own truth
Deduction, induction, abduction — the three modes of argument
The unit of proof, demonstration, and theoretical contribution
Learning through the structure of reasons, not just observations

domains.name: Firstness
hints: The mode of being that is what it is without reference to anything else
Pure possibility, feeling, and immediacy — a redness that is just red
The ontological ground of all quality and potential
What rest restores: contact with being before relation
Firstness cannot be conceived in relation; it resists language
The healing mode that asks 'what is this in itself?'
The origin point of all semiosis — quality before it signifies
A newborn's world before the subject-object split

domains.name: Secondness
hints: The mode of being that is what it is in relation to another
Brute facts, reactions, struggle, and the shock of encountering the real
The door that resists, the stone that is heavy, the hand that pushes back
What construction lifts against: the resistance of materials and gravity
Secondness is the category of effort and reaction
The sense of 'not-me' that defines the boundary of the self
Every act of building is a negotiation with secondness
The mode of cause and effect, action and reaction

domains.name: Thirdness
hints: The mode of being that brings firstness and secondness into relation
Mediation, representation, law, habit — the bridging of quality and fact
What makes a sign a sign: the triadic relation that constitutes meaning
The bond that holds when rest re-establishes connection
Thirdness is the category of continuity and growth
A habit is a thirdness: a law that governs future action
The resting ground where firstness and secondness are synthesized
The semiotic bond that makes culture, learning, and institutions possible

domains.name: Signifier
hints: The material or perceptual form of a sign — the sound 'tree' or the ink-mark 'tree'
Not the physical thing but the psychological imprint of the sound-pattern
What fabrication shapes: the material vehicle of meaning
The signifier has arbitrary relation to the signified
Signifiers differentiate: they are what they are by being not other signifiers
The entire phonetic/graphic system is a play of differences
In design, the signifier is what can be molded, carved, rendered
Creation begins by giving form to the signifier

domains.name: Signified
hints: The mental concept associated with a signifier — not the tree but the idea TREE
A purely relational unit of thought, defined by its place in the system
What study pursues: the conceptual content that a form conveys
Signifieds are not pre-existing; they are carved by language itself
The value of a signified is determined by its position in the system of differences
Learning is the acquisition of new signifieds and their relationships
The signified is inseparable from the signifier as two sides of one sheet
Concepts are not universal; they are products of specific semiotic systems

domains.name: Langue
hints: The abstract systematic structure of a language, shared by all speakers
Not any particular utterance but the rules that make utterances possible
What construction works with: the architectural grammar of a sign system
Langue is social, stable, and exists only in the collectivity
The structural foundation that enables parole to be meaningful
Working with langue means operating within rule-governed possibility
A chessboard and its rules are langue; any particular game is parole
The mode of standards, codification, and systematic design

domains.name: Parole
hints: The individual act of speech or writing — a particular utterance
Concrete, variable, personal, and situated
What bond creates in the moment of address between subjects
Parole is the actualization of langue in real communicative events
Every utterance is both constrained by langue and creative within it
The site of style, idiolect, and personal expression
Creation that happens in the living act of enunciation
Parole is where the system meets the body and the situation

domains.name: Syntagmatic
hints: The axis of combination: signs arranged in sequence, like words in a sentence
Relations are in praesentia — items actually present in the chain
What construction walks along: the linear assembly of parts into wholes
Syntagmatic relations are about 'and': this element AND that element
The horizontal axis of narrative and architectural composition
Every building is a syntagm: foundation-walls-roof in sequence
Walking through a space is a syntagmatic experience of adjacency
The mode of syntax, sequence, and co-occurrence constraints

domains.name: Paradigmatic
hints: The axis of selection: signs that could substitute for each other in a given position
Relations are in absentia — items that are not present but could be
What study works through: the virtual set of alternatives
Paradigmatic relations are about 'or': this element OR that element
The vertical axis of choice, metaphor, and classification
Every word choice activates a paradigm and suppresses its alternatives
Working in the paradigmatic mode means comparing options systematically
The mode of taxonomy, opposition, and commutation tests

domains.name: Synchronic
hints: The study of a language system at a single point in time, frozen as a state
Ignores history to reveal the structure of relations that make meaning now
What healing rest into: the present system of relations without temporal flux
Synchronic is the axis of coexistence and mutual definition
A moment of equilibrium where the structure is fully present
Rest in the synchronic mode means dwelling in the present configuration
The snapshot that reveals structural truth that diachrony obscures
The mode of diagnosis: understanding a system as it is now

domains.name: Diachronic
hints: The study of language change across time — phonetic shifts, semantic drift
Individual events of change that alter the system over generations
What fabrication walks through: the temporal layerings of form
Diachronic is the axis of transformation, evolution, and becoming
A language is never stable; diachrony is the reality of living systems
Walking through the history of a form reveals its sedimented meanings
Every material carries its diachronic traces — wear, patina, repair
The mode of genealogy, etymological depth, and transformative process

domains.name: Denotation
hints: The first-order, literal, obvious meaning of a sign
What a dictionary captures: the stable referential function
What study works with before interpretation begins
Denotation is the 'what it is' before 'what it means'
Barthes later argued denotation is just the most naturalized connotation
The supposedly neutral layer that ideology works through
Working with denotation means establishing shared factual ground
The mode of description, documentation, and literal reading

domains.name: Connotation
hints: The second-order, culturally inflected meanings that cluster around a sign
Not what a word means but what it evokes, suggests, or implies
What fabrication creates: the layered texture of associations
Connotation is where ideology and cultural values operate
A photograph denotes a man in uniform; it connotes military honor
The domain of style, tone, and cultural coding
Creating through connotation means engineering associative resonance
The mode of poetry, branding, and indirect expression

domains.name: Myth
hints: A second-order semiological system that turns history into nature
Myth makes contingent cultural constructs seem eternal and inevitable
What bond learns to recognize: the stories that hold a culture together
Myth is depoliticized speech: it strips a sign of its historical contingency
A black soldier saluting the French flag — Barthes' paradigmatic myth
Myth is not defined by its object but by how it speaks of that object
Learning myth means uncovering the buried ideological work in everyday signs
The mode of collective belief systems and their naturalizing power

domains.name: Code
hints: A system of conventions that makes a text intelligible within a culture
Not the linguistic code but the cultural codes that govern interpretation
What construction works within: the rules that structure intelligibility
Barthes identified five codes in S/Z: hermeneutic, proairetic, semantic, symbolic, cultural
Every text is woven from multiple, often contradictory codes
Working with codes means both using them and revealing their operation
The mode of genre convention, narrative structure, and readability
Codes are what make meaning systematic rather than idiosyncratic

domains.name: Writerly Text
hints: A text that demands the reader become a producer of meaning, not a consumer
Open, plural, indeterminate — resists a single interpretation
What bond creates when it invites co-authorship of meaning
The writerly text is not a thing but a practice of reading
It breaks the reader's comfortable passive consumption
Creating a writerly text means building generative ambiguity
The mode of avant-garde literature that foregrounds its own textuality
A bond formed through the shared work of making meaning

domains.name: Readerly Text
hints: A text that offers a closed, determinate meaning for passive consumption
Follows conventions, confirms expectations, satisfies the desire for clarity
What study learns from: texts designed to transmit information efficiently
The readerly is the realm of product, not production
Most popular literature, journalism, and technical writing is readerly
Learning from a readerly text means extracting its intended message
The mode of transparency where language seems to vanish before its content
A bond of comfortable familiarity rather than creative participation

domains.name: Punctum
hints: The accidental detail in a photograph that pierces, wounds, or pricks the viewer
Not the culturally coded studium but a personal, unnamable puncture
What healing lifts when a wound is acknowledged and integrated
The punctum is involuntary; it chooses you, you do not choose it
It expands beyond the photograph's frame into the viewer's own life
The element that breaks the studium's comfortable distance
Lifting the punctum into awareness is the work of mourning
The mode of singular, traumatic, and transformative encounter

domains.name: Studium
hints: The general, culturally informed interest a viewer brings to a photograph
Polite, distanced, analytical — the domain of education and taste
What study walks through: the culturally coded surface of an image
The studium is 'that very wide field of unconcerned desire'
It requires the viewer to be culturally competent but emotionally detached
Walking through the studium means recognizing historical and cultural references
The mode of informed observation without personal risk
Studium is what binds a community of educated viewers

domains.name: Unlimited Semiosis
hints: The process by which every sign refers to another sign in an endless chain
No sign has fixed meaning; each interpretant becomes a new representamen
What bond creates when it refuses to close meaning down
Unlimited semiosis is not chaos but the very condition of signification
The encyclopedia is the global network that unlimited semiosis traverses
Every dictionary is a closed loop of signs defining signs defining signs
Creating in the mode of unlimited semiosis means embracing interpretive openness
The epistemological ground of hermeneutics and textual plurality

domains.name: Encyclopedia
hints: The sum total of all interpretive habits and cultural knowledge in a semiotic system
Not a structured dictionary but a disorganized, potentially infinite network
What study learns to navigate: the labyrinthine web of cultural associations
The encyclopedia is a regulative hypothesis, not a retrievable object
Every act of interpretation draws on an implicit encyclopedia
Learning is the progressive expansion and refinement of one's encyclopedia
The encyclopedia includes not just words but images, practices, and institutions
The mode of cultural competence, erudition, and intertextual knowledge

domains.name: Intentio Operis
hints: The intention of the text itself, neither author's nor reader's but the work's strategy
An interpretive hypothesis that the text encourages through its internal structure
What fabrication works to embed: the text's own interpretive constraints
The intentio operis is not fixed but is the range of plausible interpretations
It resists both the intentional fallacy and radical interpretive license
Working with intentio operis means designing textual affordances
The mode of authorial strategy encoded in textual structure
A text is an 'open work' that nonetheless has boundaries of coherence

domains.name: Aberrant Decoding
hints: The gap between the encoded meaning and the decoded meaning across cultures
A message sent with one code is received through a different code
What study walks into: the space between intention and interpretation
Aberrant decoding is not error; it is the normal condition of cross-cultural communication
Eco analyzed this through television: the same broadcast means differently in different homes
Walking through aberrant decodings reveals the hidden codes of both sender and receiver
The mode of cultural translation and its inevitable frictions
Every act of global communication must anticipate aberrant decoding

domains.name: Iconism
hints: The recognition that iconic resemblance is itself culturally coded
Even 'natural' likeness (a photograph) operates through learned conventions
What fabrication creates when it produces culturally legible likeness
Eco argued iconism is not natural but a set of conventional stimulus-properties
A portrait resembles its subject only for those trained in Western perspective
Creating iconically means encoding visual conventions into material form
The mode of diagrammatic reasoning and analogical representation
Iconism is the intersection of perceptual similarity and cultural coding

domains.name: Ratio Facilis
hints: Production where the token conforms to a pre-existing type or model
The type precedes and governs the creation of the token
What construction creates when building to specification
Ratio facilis is the mode of templates, blueprints, and patterns
Most industrial production follows ratio facilis
Creating through ratio facilis means faithful instantiation of a rule
The mode of standardization, replication, and typological regularity
Ratio difficilis would be the inverse: the type must be invented from the token

domains.name: Referential
hints: The function oriented toward the context or referent — conveying information
The dominant function in scientific and technical discourse
What study works with: facts, data, and propositional content
The referential function is denotative and cognitive
In Jakobson's model, every message has multiple functions but one is dominant
Working referentially means prioritizing clarity and accuracy
The mode of reports, documentation, and evidence-based communication
The referential function answers 'what is being talked about?'

domains.name: Emotive
hints: The function oriented toward the addresser — expressing attitude or emotion
Interjections, tone of voice, and affective markers
What healing lifts: the expressive dimension of the self
The emotive function reveals the speaker's subjective stance
Even referential statements carry emotive coloring through intonation
Lifting the emotive dimension means acknowledging feeling as meaning
The mode of poetry, confession, and authentic self-expression
The emotive function answers 'who is speaking and how do they feel?'

domains.name: Conative
hints: The function oriented toward the addressee — seeking to persuade or command
Imperatives, vocatives, and rhetorical appeals
What bond walks toward when it seeks response from the other
The conative function is most naked in commands: 'Drink!'
It reveals the social relationship between addresser and addressee
Walking conatively means moving the other toward action
The mode of persuasion, instruction, and behavioral influence
The conative function answers 'what effect is sought in the receiver?'

domains.name: Phatic
hints: The function oriented toward maintaining the channel of communication
Small talk, greetings, back-channels — 'Hello? Are you still there?'
What bond rests in: the pure maintenance of connection without message
The phatic function checks that the channel is open and working
It is the first function infants learn — the pleasure of connection itself
Resting in the phatic means attending to relationship over content
The mode of ritual exchange, social bonding, and presence
The phatic function answers 'is this connection still alive?'

domains.name: Metalingual
hints: The function oriented toward the code itself — language talking about language
Definitions, glosses, grammar lessons — 'By 'metalingual' I mean...'
What study learns when it reflects on its own tools
The metalingual function is essential for learning any code
Every dictionary is an extended exercise of the metalingual function
Learning through the metalingual means understanding the system of rules
The mode of pedagogy, grammar, and philosophical reflection on language
The metalingual function answers 'what code is being used and how does it work?'

domains.name: Poetic
hints: The function oriented toward the message itself — its form, sound, and texture
The poetic function foregrounds the signifier over the signified
What fabrication creates when it makes the medium part of the message
The poetic function projects the principle of equivalence from selection into combination
Rhyme, alliteration, parallelism — patterns that make form noticeable
Creating poetically means crafting the sensory experience of the message
The mode of literature, advertising, and any discourse that values how over what
The poetic function answers 'how is this message shaped and why does that matter?'

domains.name: Actantial Model
hints: A structural model of narrative with six actantial roles: Sender, Object, Receiver, Helper, Subject, Opponent
Not characters but functions: an actant can be a person, thing, or abstraction
What construction creates: the structural skeleton of a story
The actantial model reveals the deep grammar underlying all narratives
Subject desires Object; Sender dispatches; Receiver benefits; Helper aids; Opponent obstructs
Creating an actantial model means mapping the force dynamics of a situation
The mode of scenario planning, myth analysis, and narrative design
Every quest, conspiracy, or project can be mapped onto actantial roles

domains.name: Semiotic Square
hints: A logical tool for mapping the deep structure of oppositions in any sign system
Based on the contrary and contradictory relations between terms
What bond learns when it understands how meaning emerges from opposition
The square has four positions: A, not-A, not-not-A, and the neutral term
Used to analyze everything from kinship systems to political ideologies
Learning the semiotic square reveals hidden logical relations in binary oppositions
The mode of structural analysis and dialectical thinking
The square transforms simple binaries into complex systems of implication

domains.name: Isotopy
hints: The recurrence of semantic categories that makes a text coherent and readable
A level of meaning at which a text maintains semantic homogeneity
What study works with when establishing a text's unified interpretation
Multiple isotopies can coexist in the same text — the basis of polysemy
A pun operates at the intersection of two isotopies
Working with isotopy means tracing the semantic threads that bind a discourse
The mode of discourse analysis, thematic criticism, and coherence testing
Isotopy explains why some readings feel consistent and others arbitrary

domains.name: Narrative Program
hints: The basic unit of narrative syntax: a transformation from one state to another
S → (F → S) : A subject acts to transform the state of another subject
What fabrication walks through: the sequence of transformations in a process
A narrative program involves a subject, an action, and a change of state
Complex narratives chain multiple programs into narrative sequences
Walking the narrative program means tracing causal-transformational chains
The mode of process modeling, scenario mapping, and action analysis
Every making is a narrative program: raw material transformed into artifact
